description.task: Iterate over an ArrayList of cities using three different approaches: traditional for loop, for-each loop, and Iterator.
instructions: Method 1: for (int i = 0; i < cities.size(); i++) { System.out.println(cities.get(i)); }
Method 3: Create an Iterator<String>, use hasNext() and next() to print each city.
All three should print the same 4 cities in order.
description.hint: Iterator: Iterator<String> it = cities.iterator(); while (it.hasNext()) { System.out.println(it.next()); }
description.expectedOutput:
--- Index for loop ---
Paris
Tokyo
New York
Sydney
--- For-each loop ---
Paris
Tokyo
New York
Sydney
--- Iterator ---
Paris
Tokyo
New York
Sydney

description.task: Use an Iterator to safely remove elements from an ArrayList while iterating — remove all numbers less than 10.
description.context: Modifying a list while iterating with a for-each loop causes ConcurrentModificationException. The Iterator.remove() method is the safe way to remove during iteration.
instructions: Inside the while loop, get the next element with it.next().
If the element is less than 10, call it.remove() to safely remove it.
Do NOT modify the list directly inside the loop.
Do NOT change the println statements outside the loop.
description.hint: int val = it.next(); if (val < 10) { it.remove(); } — Never call list.remove() inside a for-each loop; always use the Iterator's own remove().

description.task: Iterate over a HashMap using entrySet() to print both keys and values, then use values() to calculate the total.
description.context: HashMap iteration is typically done via keySet(), values(), or entrySet(). entrySet() is most efficient when you need both key and value.
instructions: Use for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> entry : scores.entrySet()) to iterate.
Print each entry as 'Alice: 88' using entry.getKey() and entry.getValue().
Use for (int s : scores.values()) { total += s; } to compute the total.
Print total and average (order of name-score pairs may vary due to HashMap).
description.hint: Map.Entry is part of java.util.Map. Import both Map and HashMap. entrySet() provides key-value pairs as Entry objects.
description.expectedOutput:
--- Scores ---
Alice: 88
Bob: 75
Carol: 92
Dave: 65
Total: 320
Average: 80.00
